rationale: Title Y is cleaner and more PACE-like: it is slightly shorter, avoids the heavier phrase “S-ICD implantation,” and “implant outcomes” is more specific than the broader “outcomes.” Both titles clearly identify the population and study design, though Title X is marginally more informative by specifying “retrospective.”
loser_weakness: Title X is more cumbersome and less polished, mainly because “Subcutaneous Implantable Cardioverter-Defibrillator Implantation” feels repetitive and “Safety and Outcomes” is broad.
